# terrible_community_general_consul_session (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `behavior` (String) The optional behavior that can be attached to the session when it is created. This controls the behavior when a session is invalidated.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `checks` (String) Checks that are used to verify the session health. If all the checks fail, the session is invalidated and any locks associated with the session are released and can be acquired once the associated lock delay has expired.
- `datacenter` (String) The name of the datacenter in which the session exists or should be created.
- `delay` (Number) The optional lock delay that can be attached to the session when it is created. Locks for invalidated sessions ar blocked from being acquired until this delay has expired. Durations are in seconds.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `name` (String) The name that should be associated with the session. Required when `state=node` is used.
- `node` (String) The name of the node that with which the session is associated. By default this is the name of the agent.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Whether the session should be present, in other words it should be created if it does not exist, or absent, removed if present. If created, the `id` for the session is returned in the output. If `absent`, `id` is required to remove the session. Info for a single session, all the sessions for a node or all available sessions can be retrieved by specifying `info`, `node` or `list` for the `state`; for `node` or `info`, the node `name` or session `id` is required as parameter.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `token` (String)
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `ttl` (Number) Specifies the duration of a session in seconds (between 10 and 86400).

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
